title: Extraction Manifest
description: The extraction manifest every source-format adapter (gutenberg_epub, plain_text, custom) must produce -- the EXACT same shape regardless of adapter, validated immediately after extraction by each adapter's OWN extractor (extract.py.template for gutenberg_epub/plain_text; the co-designed custom extractor at scripts/custom_extractors/<value> for custom -- see references/source-format-adapters/custom.md) using the real jsonschema.Draft202012Validator (never the hand-rolled checker validate_draft.py/segpack.py use elsewhere). Encodes the block-ID/order_index model (blocks{}, keyed by block id, each block carrying its own order_index -- the single global reading-order axis spine[]/segments[]/footnotes[]/verse.store all reference by id rather than duplicating), the reading-order spine (spine[]), the translatable-unit inventory (segments[], EXPLICITLY inclusive of translate-decision FRONTBACK:{id} units and not just body content -- a translate-decision frontback unit gets its own segments[] entry with kind:"frontback" and is segpacked/dispatched/reviewed/ledgered through the exact same generic pipeline as a body segment), the footnote anchor/definition table (footnotes[]), the verse-placeholder inventory (verse.store), the full front/back-matter disposition inventory (frontback[]), and the extraction provenance hashes (generation_hashes.source_extraction_hash/.source_input_hash) alongside the raw source-file list they are derived from (source_inputs[]). CROSS-REFERENCE INVARIANT THIS SCHEMA CANNOT EXPRESS (checked PROCEDURALLY instead, never schema-expressible -- a cross-array key-set comparison is outside JSON Schema's reach): every frontback[] entry with decision:"translate" MUST have a matching id in segments[] (as that segment's own `seg` value); every frontback[] entry with decision:"regenerate" or decision:"omit" MUST NOT appear in segments[] at all. The producing extractor's own round-trip self-check suite (extract.py.template for gutenberg_epub/plain_text; the co-designed custom extractor, or a documented equivalent, for custom) enforces this as a FATAL named failure in EITHER direction (a translate-decision frontback unit missing from segments[], or a regenerate/omit-decision unit that leaked into segments[] anyway) -- this schema only validates that segments[] and frontback[] are each individually well-shaped, not that the two arrays agree with each other.

blocks.description: Every extracted content unit, keyed by its own block id (e.g. "HEAD:seg02", "PARA:seg02:0001", "VERSE:seg02:0012", "FN:1", "FRONTBACK:fm01"). The id used as the object key and the block record's own `id` field must agree (checked by the producing extractor's own uniqueness/bijection self-checks -- extract.py.template for gutenberg_epub/plain_text, the custom extractor for custom -- not by this schema).

additionalProperties.description: One block: order_index is this block's position on the single global reading-order axis spine/segments/footnotes/verse.store all key off of.

type.description: Adapter-defined block-type tag (e.g. HEAD/PARA/VERSE/FN/FRONTBACK for gutenberg_epub). Deliberately not a fixed enum -- plain_text introduces its own block types via extract.py.template's # ADAPT-POINT: classification hooks (#62); a custom adapter defines its own block-type tags directly in its own standalone extractor (scripts/custom_extractors/<value>), which does not extend extract.py.template or use its ADAPT-POINTs at all.

seg.description: Owning segment id, or null for a block not (yet) assigned to any segment -- e.g. an unresolved FRONTBACK block before classify_frontback_block runs.

source_html.description: Raw source markup for this block. Absent for adapters with no markup concept (e.g. a plain_text source with no HTML to preserve).

sha1.description: sha1 of this block's own content, as assigned by the producing extractor (extract.py.template's generic core for gutenberg_epub/plain_text; the custom extractor's own hashing for custom).

fnrefs.description: Footnote numbers this block anchors, if any.

decision.description: Present only on FRONTBACK-type blocks -- mirrors the matching frontback[] entry's own decision. Never present on body block types.

reason.description: Present only on FRONTBACK-type blocks -- human-readable justification for `decision`.

origin.description: Present only on FRONTBACK-type blocks -- e.g. "front-matter"/"back-matter" provenance tag.

spine.description: Ordered list of source-file/spine-item classifications the producing extractor resolved (extract.py.template's classify_spine_item hook for gutenberg_epub, each overridable per-file via adapter_config.gutenberg_epub.spine_overrides; the custom extractor's own equivalent logic for custom).

pos.description: Position of this item in the source's own native spine/file order (never re-ranked -- order_index on blocks is the derived global axis, this is the adapter's raw input order).

idref.description: Adapter-native item identifier (e.g. an EPUB spine idref). Absent for adapters with no such concept.

klass.description: Content classification for this spine item -- matches profile.yml's adapter_config.gutenberg_epub.spine_overrides value domain.

segments.description: The full translatable-unit inventory -- EXPLICITLY inclusive of translate-decision FRONTBACK:{id} units (kind:"frontback"), not just ordinary body content (kind:"body"). A frontback-kind entry's `seg` value equals the matching frontback[] entry's own `id` (the cross-reference invariant this schema cannot itself enforce -- see the top-level description).

seg.description: This segment's own id -- an ordinary body segment id (e.g. "seg02") or, for a translate-decision frontback unit, the matching FRONTBACK:{id} string. Enforced at runtime by the consuming scripts (validate_seg); this pattern documents the path/shell-safety contract.

kind.description: Copied straight into segpack.schema.json's own `kind` field at segpack-generation time, never re-derived.

block_ids.description: Ordered block ids (keys into the top-level blocks{} object) that make up this segment.

word_count.description: Drives the blocking no_segment_exceeds_max_words self-check against profile.yml's max_segment_words -- extract.py.template's own check for gutenberg_epub/plain_text; a documented equivalent the custom extractor must implement for custom (see references/source-format-adapters/custom.md). Also independently re-derived by validate_extraction.py's managed gate for every format.

footnotes.description: The footnote anchor/definition table. Empty under apparatus_policy: body_refs_only (no FN:{N} block extracted/carried at all -- see body_ref_markers[] on the owning segpack block instead) and under apparatus_policy: omit_apparatus (anchor stripped, nothing left behind).

n.description: Footnote number, shared by the anchor sentinel and the FN:{N} definition block id.

anchor_block.description: Block id (key into blocks{}) carrying the ⟦FNREF_N⟧ anchor.

anchor_seg.description: Segment id (matches a segments[] entry's own `seg`) owning the anchor block.

def_block.description: Block id (key into blocks{}) carrying this footnote's own FN:{N} definition.

frontback.description: Full inventory of every front/back-matter block the producing extractor decomposed (extract.py.template's classify_frontback_block hook for gutenberg_epub, each overridable per-element via adapter_config.gutenberg_epub.frontback_overrides; the custom extractor's own equivalent logic for custom), each resolved to translate/regenerate/omit. A translate-decision entry gets its own segments[] entry (kind:"frontback", `seg` equal to this entry's `id`); a regenerate/omit-decision entry does NOT -- see the top-level description for the full cross-reference invariant, checked procedurally by the producing extractor, never by this schema.

reason.description: Human-readable justification for `decision` (e.g. "Project Gutenberg boilerplate header", "title-page / front-matter text").

text_head.description: Leading excerpt of this block's plain text, for human review of the decision -- may be empty for a purely structural/decorative block.

verse.description: The verse-placeholder inventory and summary counts the verse-detection loop (union of container-class and bare-line/stanza fallback) produced.

store.description: One entry per detected verse node -- the authoritative source for the per-block verse-placeholder bijection check every verse_policy.mode enforces identically (see references/verse-policy.md).

vid.description: This verse's unique key (e.g. "V001") -- the same key the ⟦VERSE_{vid}_{shortsha}⟧ placeholder and the segpack/draft verses{} container reference.

placeholder.description: The exact sentinel string baked into the owning block's plain text in place of this verse's content.

mount.description: How this verse node attaches to its parent block (e.g. "block" for a whole dedicated block, adapter-defined otherwise).

parent_block.description: Block id (key into blocks{}) whose plain text carries this verse's placeholder.

source_inputs.description: Every source file path this extractor actually read, in read order. For gutenberg_epub/plain_text this is always [source.path]; for custom (which may consume multiple files) this is the sole authoritative full-file-list generation_hashes.source_input_hash actually hashes.

generation_hashes.description: Extraction-provenance stamps written by the two-phase cache_key.py write immediately after extraction completes -- particle_config_hash and derivation_bundle_hash are NEVER stamped here (they live in canon.json's own generation_hashes instead, stamped at the glossary-pass merge step).

source_extraction_hash.description: sha1 of canonical JSON {format, adapter_config: <only the one sub-block matching the resolved format>} concatenated with the resolved extractor file's own raw bytes.

source_input_hash.description: sha1 of canonical JSON {source_path, source_bytes_sha1} derived from source_inputs[].

heading_types.description: Adapter-declared block-type tags that are headings (in addition to the always-heading built-in 'HEAD'). A custom extractor lists its own heading tags here (e.g. ['SIMAN','PEREK']); the shipped gutenberg_epub adapter emits 'HEAD' and need not set it.

heading_levels.description: Optional per-block-type markdown heading level (1-6) for types listed in heading_types (or the built-in 'HEAD'). A type absent from this map, or an absent map, renders at level 2 -- byte-identical to pre-1.12.0 output.
